name: Easy Chicken Stir Fry
recipeIngredient: 2 green onions (cut lengthwise, then diagonal into 2-inch pieces)
3 chicken breast halves (cut into thin strips)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the broccoli into florets, peel and cut the stalks on a diagonal into 1/4-inch thick slices. Cut the sweet red pepper into 1-inch squares. Halve the green onions lengthwise, then cut into 2-inch pieces. Cut the chicken into thin strips.
2. Whisk together 1/4 cup of chicken stock, soy sauce, cornstarch, oyster sauce, sherry, sesame oil, and chili paste in a bowl; set aside.
3. Heat a wok or deep skillet over high heat. Add 2 tablespoons vegetable oil; heat for 30 seconds, swirling to coat the pan. Stir-fry half the chicken until no longer pink inside, about 3-4 minutes, then remove and set aside. Repeat with remaining chicken, adding some of the remaining oil if necessary. Add to the reserved chicken.
4. Add the remaining oil to the wok. Stir-fry garlic and ginger for 10 seconds or until fragrant. Add broccoli and pepper; stir-fry for 1 minute. Add green onions; stir-fry for 30 seconds. Pour in the remaining stock; cover and steam, stirring once, for 2 minutes or until broccoli is tender-crisp.
5. Stir chicken back into wok. Push to one side of the pan. Pour the soy mixture into the center of the wok; cook, stirring, for 1-2 minutes or until thickened. Stir chicken mixture into sauce until coated. Sprinkle with cashew nuts.
